angle: A host's phone lights up at 2 AM with a guest question, and it's already been answered. The stress of always-on availability, dissolved.
brief: A dark bedroom in the small hours. A phone glows on the nightstand, a guest message arriving and resolving on its own while the host stays asleep. Warm light creeps toward dawn and the room settles into calm. The read: autonomous hosting means you are off the hook without lifting a finger. Hostie answers guests the moment they message, so always-on stops being your problem. Cinematic, intimate, product-in-world; the copy rides as caption or overlay, never burned into the generated frame.

video.prompt: Cinematic, intimate low-light interior. A smartphone resting face-up on a wooden nightstand in a dark bedroom, screen softly glowing. A notification gently appears on the screen and resolves on its own, the glow easing. Warm ambient light slowly creeps in from a window as night turns toward dawn; the room settles into calm. A person sleeps peacefully, out of focus in the background. Shallow depth of field, slow push-in on the phone, muted nighttime palette warming to soft amber. Photorealistic, filmic grain, gentle handheld feel. No text, no captions, no on-screen graphics, no UI text.

copy.headline: Answered before you woke up.
copy.subhead: Hostie replies to guests the moment they message, so being a host stops meaning being on call.
